title: Algorand Agent Skills
description: Supercharge your AI assistant with specialized Algorand development knowledge.
content:
# Algorand Agent Skills

**Algorand Agent Skills** are a "power pill" for your development workflow. They are specialized sets of instructions, tool configurations, and reference materials that teach AI assistants (like Claude, Cursor, or Copilot) exactly how to build on Algorand.

## What do they do?

When you load Agent Skills into your preferred AI tool, it gains expert knowledge in:

* **AlgoKit Workflows**: Initializing, testing, and deploying projects.
* **Smart Contract Languages**: Writing clean, efficient Puya TypeScript (PuyaTs) or Python.
* **Troubleshooting**: Diagnosing and fixing common Algorand errors instantly.
* **Standards**: Implementing ARC standards (like ARC-4, ARC-32, ARC-56) correctly.

## Why use them?

Instead of spending weeks reading documentation, you can describe your intent to your AI assistant, and it will use the Agent Skills to generate production-grade Algorand code that follows all best practices.

Ready to setup? Head over to the **[Quick Start](/docs/agent-skills/quick-start)** guide.

title: Quick Start
description: How to setup Algorand Agent Skills in your favorite AI tool.
content:
# Quick Start: Setting up Agent Skills

Follow these steps to integrate Algorand Agent Skills into your project.

## 1. Prerequisites

You need the **[algorand-agent-skills](https://github.com/algorand-devrel/algorand-agent-skills)** repository cloned to your machine.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/algorand-devrel/algorand-agent-skills.git
```

## 2. Setup (Per Project)

Navigate to your active project directory and copy the necessary skills and configuration files.

### For Cursor

1. Create a `.cursor/skills` directory in your project root.
2. Copy the skills:
   ```bash
   cp -r ../algorand-agent-skills/skills/* .cursor/skills/
   ```
3. Copy the MCP configuration:
   ```bash
   cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/cursor/mcp.json .cursor/mcp.json
   ```

### For Claude Code

```bash
# Copy the skills to your project
cp -r ../algorand-agent-skills/skills ./
# Copy the agent configuration
cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/claude-code/.mcp.json ./
cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/claude-code/CLAUDE.md ./
```

## 3. GitHub PAT Setup

All skills require access to search code examples via GitHub.

1. Create a [GitHub Personal Access Token](https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens).
2. Set the `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable:
   ```bash
   export GITHUB_TOKEN="your_token_here"
   ```

Now, your AI assistant is ready to help you build on Algorand!

title: Getting Started
description: How to start and where to begin your Algorand journey.
content:
# Getting Started with Algorand

New to Algorand? This page will guide you through the essential resources and point you in the right direction to start building.

## Where to Start?

The Algorand ecosystem is rich with resources. Here are the most important entry points:

1. **[Algorand Developer Portal](https://developer.algorand.org/)**: The source of truth for all technical documentation, API references, and in-depth tutorials.
2. **[AlgoKit](https://github.com/algorandfoundation/algokit-cli)**: The official development toolkit. If you're building on Algorand, you'll be using AlgoKit.
3. **[Discord Community](https://discord.gg/algorand)**: Join thousands of other developers to ask questions and get support.

## Your Path to Development

Your journey typically follows these steps:

1. **Understand the Basics**: Learn about Accounts, Assets (ASAs), and Transactions.
2. **Install the Tooling**: Set up your development environment.
3. **Write your First Contract**: Use Python or TypeScript to logic on-chain.
4. **Connect a Frontend**: Build a user interface that interacts with your contracts.

Next, let's look at what you need to install on your machine to get moving.

**[Setup & Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation)**

title: Setup & Installation
description: Watch the getting started video and install the necessary development tools.
content:
# Setup & Installation

Follow this guide to get your machine ready for Algorand development.

## Watch the Getting Started Video

Before you start installing, watch this overview to understand the workflow:

<iframe width="100%" height="450" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/HrZUe5P4mUY" title="Algorand Getting Started" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen className="rounded-xl border shadow-sm" />

***

## What to Install

To build on Algorand, you'll need the following core tools:

### 1. Python 3.10+

Most Algorand tooling, including AlgoKit and Puya Py, is built with Python.

* [Download Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/)

### 2. Docker Desktop

Required for running an Algorand "LocalNet" (a private private network on your machine).

* [Install Docker](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/)

### 3. AlgoKit CLI

AlgoKit is the primary interface for starting projects, running local nodes, and deploying contracts.

```bash
# Install via pipx (recommended)
pipx install algokit

# Verify installation
algokit --version
```

### 4. Node.js (Optional but Recommended)

Required if you are building React/Next.js frontends or using the TypeScript SDK.

* [Install Node.js](https://nodejs.org/)

***

## Spin up your First Node

Once Docker and AlgoKit are installed, you can start a local Algorand node:

```bash
algokit localnet start
```

This gives you a functional Algorand network with pre-funded accounts to test your applications for free.

title: Welcome to Algorand JNTU
description: Technical onboarding documentation for the Algorand JNTU ecosystem.
content:
# Algorand JNTU Onboarding

Welcome to the official developer onboarding portal for **Algorand JNTU**. This guide is designed to take you from zero to building production-ready decentralized applications on the Algorand blockchain.

## Why Algorand?

Algorand is a high-performance, carbon-neutral blockchain that solves the "Blockchain Trilemma" of security, scalability, and decentralization. With its **Pure Proof of Stake (PPoS)** consensus, it offers:

* **Instant Finality**: Transactions are confirmed in seconds.
* **Low Fees**: Scalable infrastructure with minimal costs.
* **Powerful Logic**: Write smart contracts in Python or TypeScript.

## How to navigate these docs

Use the sidebar to explore the following sections:

* **[Getting Started](/docs/getting-started)**: Learn the fundamentals and set up your local development environment.
* **[Agent Skills](/docs/agent-skills)**: Power up your AI assistant with specialized Algorand development knowledge.
* **[Starters & Templates](/docs/starters)**: Jumpstart your project with pre-configured frontend and custodian wallet templates.

Ready to dive in? Head over to **[How to Start](/docs/getting-started)**.

title: Starters & Templates
description: Pre-configured templates to kickstart your Algorand development.
content:
# Starters & Templates

Jumpstart your development with these high-quality templates for frontend and user onboarding.

## 1. Connect Wallet Starter V2

The definitive starter for building React/Next.js applications on Algorand. It includes the logic for connecting wallets like Pera, Defly, and Kibisis out of the box.

### [Download on GitHub](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2)

**Setup Steps:**

1. Clone: `git clone https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2.git`
2. Install: `npm install`
3. Run: `npm run dev`

***

## 2. Kyra Custodian Wallet (Onboarding)

Perfect for onboarding users who have never touched blockchain. This template allows users to sign in with **Google**—no Pera wallet required to start.

### [Custodian Starter on GitHub](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2-custodian)

**Why use Kyra?**

* **Zero Friction**: Users get a functional Algorand wallet just by logging in with their Google account.
* **KYRA Ecosystem**: The wallet is securely managed at [kyra.algocraft.fun](https://kyra.algocraft.fun).
* **Mass Adoption**: Ideal for building web3 apps with a traditional web2 user experience.

***

## 3. Algorand Connect UI

A library of beautiful, pre-designed components for Algorand wallet interactions.

* **Live Demo**: [docs.algocraft.fun](https://docs.algocraft.fun)
